name: Jean-Paul Sartre
abstract: Jean-Paul Charles Aymard Sartre (, US also ; French: [saʁtʁ]; 21 June 1905 – 15 April 1980) was a French philosopher, playwright, novelist, screenwriter, political activist, biographer, and literary critic, considered a leading figure in 20th-century French philosophy and Marxism. Sartre was one of the key figures in the philosophy of ex…

about_quotes.speaker: Abbie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, I read an article by a man called, his name was Jean Paul Sartre... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, Lawn Party said, well, uh, gonna have a bone dance.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy told me that he was engaged to do a cabaret scene in a movie being filmed called 'The Respectful Prostitute', from a Jean-Paul Sartre novel.

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: I remember that when I met you, you wanted me to read Jean-Paul Sartre... I was really impressed!
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
